title: Diagnose and recover FP300 bathroom sensor Matter outage
description: Investigated 12+ hour bathroom FP300 (Aqara Presence Multi-Sensor) Matter outage. Root cause: Node 13 failed subscriptions after 30-second reset attempt. Cleaned up stale device registry entries, removed old node identifiers, renamed entity IDs back to bathroom_presence_sensor_* for automation compatibility. Re-paired device which became Node 15. Identified secondary issue: bathroom_lamp entity goes unavailable intermittently, blocking lighting automation. FP300 is now live and reporting state; lighting trigger blocked by lamp unavailability.
close_reason: Session complete. FP300 recovered and re-paired as Node 15, entity IDs restored for automation compatibility. Secondary lamp unavailability issue identified for follow-up.

title: Ember Mug 2: restore missing automations and integrations
description: Recently replaced Ember Mug with Ember Mug 2 and removed Ember Travel Mug. New mug is in mobile dashboard but automations/integrations related to the mug are missing. Need to identify what integrations broke and restore them.
close_reason: Updated both coffee alert automations to use Ember Mug 2 entity IDs. Both automations verified enabled on server.

title: Matter/Thread mesh instability: 26 device dropouts in 7 days
description: Bathroom presence sensor missed motion detection on Mar 16. Investigation revealed systemic Matter/Thread mesh instability affecting all 10 Matter devices, not just the bathroom sensor.
notes:
## Investigation Findings (2026-03-16)

### Trigger
Walked into bathroom, presence sensor didn't detect motion, lights didn't turn on.

### Root Cause Analysis
Not a single-device issue. 26 dropout events across all Matter devices in 7 days:
- 4 fabric-wide drops (all devices simultaneously)
- 22 partial drops (1-2 devices losing Thread mesh connectivity)

### Device Dropout Frequency (7 days)
- Living room light (Nanoleaf): 14x drops, avg 15-20 min each
- Kitchen counter switch (ThirdReality): 11x
- Laundry room switch (ThirdReality): 10x
- Nuki Smart Lock: 7x
- Bathroom presence sensor (Aqara FP300): 4x (most stable, only fabric-wide events)

### Fabric-Wide Drops (all devices at same millisecond)
- Mar 11, 9:50 AM ET
- Mar 12, 3:23 AM ET (watchdog triggered, auto-restarted Matter server)
- Mar 15, 9:41 PM ET
- Mar 16, 8:57 AM ET (coincided with WiFi blip from eero guest network change)

### Architecture
- All devices on Matter integration, single config entry
- Thread border routers: 3 Apple devices (Living Room AppleTV, Kitchen HomePod, Bedroom HomePod) + Nanoleaf Shapes B178
- eero has its own separate Thread network (eero-thread-a2f5) — NOT used by HA devices
- HA Matter devices on Apple Thread network (MyHome1620262151, channel 25)
- Matter Server v8.3.0 (current), running as HA add-on

### Issues Found
1. **Matter server add-on watchdog: DISABLED** — if process crashes, stays down
2. **Custom watchdog automation only catches stuck nodes** — triggered once in 7 days (sensor.matter_subscription_failures), missed 25 other dropout events
3. **Bathroom Nanoleaf firmware outdated** — 3.5.10 vs 4.1.3on other Nanoleafs (update via Nanoleaf app only, no HA OTA entity)
4. **Living room Nanoleaf is worst offender** (14 drops) — likely a Thread router that other devices route through
5. **ThirdReality switches drop together** — suggests shared Thread routing path

### Recommended Actions
1. Enable watchdog on Matter server add-on
2. Update bathroom Nanoleaf firmware via Nanoleaf app
3. Investigate living room Nanoleaf instability (possible Thread router issue)
4. Check Thread topology in Apple Home app
5. Build better monitoring automation for all unavailable transitions
6. Consider whether partial drops (15-30 min) are Matter server subscription issues or actual Thread mesh partitions

title: Fix nighttime bathroom-to-bedroom lighting
description: f16ce57 nighttime bathroom presence change is not behaving as intended. Make the automation use an explicit choose default and deploy/validate.
close_reason: Fixed f16ce57: Changed nested choose fallback from empty conditions to explicit default. Deployed to HA and restarted. Ready for end-to-end testing.

title: Adjust bathroom presence lighting for nighttime
description: Modify bedroom_bathroom_presence automation: after sundown, bathroom presence triggers only bedroom_lamp at 30% brightness instead of full bedroom lights
close_reason: Deployed nighttime conditional logic: sun elevation < 0 triggers bedroom_lamp 30% only, else full bedroom_lights

title: Fix Music Assistant play_media targeting in sexy_time_mode automation
description: Music Assistant play_media service was using target: entity_id instead of data: entity_id. This caused the service to silently fail, ignoring the bedroom HomePod and falling back to default speaker (kitchen), then resuming old playlist instead of playing Hania Rani. Fixed syntax in sexy_time_mode.yaml and deployed.
close_reason: Fixed Music Assistant play_media service call syntax and deployed to HA server. Changed from target: entity_id to data: entity_id. Automation now correctly plays Hania Rani on bedroom HomePod when sexy mode activated.

title: Add sexy time mode for bedroom with bedside button
description: Reconfigure bedroom single-click button to activate sexy time mode: set bedside lamp to 10% brightness with pinkish hue, turn off overhead lights, and play music on bedside HomePod. Need to: remap button action, create/update bedroom scene or automation, verify HomePod music playback is supported (may need to explore spotify/music integration), add to mode matrix and reset logic.
close_reason: Implemented sexy_time_mode: input_boolean, scene (dim pink lamp 10%), automation handler with HomePod music resume, bedroom button remapped to cycle off→sexy→off, mode conflict matrix for working/cleaning modes, reset_all_modes, mode_light_watchers

title: Add lights out mode for living room and kitchen
description: Create a new mode that disables presence-based lighting reactions in living room and kitchen. Sometimes lights should just stay off regardless of motion/presence. Need to add: input_boolean helper, automation handler, integration with mode conflict matrix, and top-line toggle on mobile dashboard.
close_reason: Implemented lights_out_mode: input_boolean, automation, guards on kitchen/living room presence automations, mode conflict matrix for all relevant modes, reset_all_modes script, mobile dashboard toggle

title: Battery status tab on mobile dashboard
description: Add a new view/tab to the mobile dashboard showing device battery levels, matching the battery card already on the desktop dashboard.
close_reason: Added Batteries tab to mobile dashboard with all 7 device battery sensors.

title: Battery status dashboard card
description: Add a dashboard card showing all device battery levels at a glance. Battery Notes integration provides battery_type and battery_voltage entities but no dashboard visualization exists.
close_reason: Battery card added to desktop dashboard right column showing all 7 device batteries with icons.

title: Low battery alert automation
description: Create an automation that notifies when any device battery drops below a threshold. Battery Notes integration is installed and creating battery entities but no alerts exist.
close_reason: Automation created on HA server and local YAML stored. Alerts on 7 device batteries < 20%.

title: Document default agent co-author footer
description: Add repo instruction for default agent co-author footer in AGENTS.md so future commits use consistent attribution without prompting.
close_reason: Added default agent co-author footer to AGENTS.md for future commits.

title: Fix sexy_time_mode music playback on bedroom HomePod
description: Currently uses media_player.media_play which only resumes paused content — HomePod was idle so nothing started. Need to decide on a music source and implement actual playback. Options explored so far: internet radio streams (user declined), Spotify integration (not set up). User wants a different approach — needs discussion on what to actually play.
close_reason: Implemented using music_assistant.play_media targeting media_player.bedroom_3 (MA entity for bedroom HomePod). Plays Hania Rani in radio mode at 35% volume on sexy mode ON.

title: remove travel mug card from mobile dashboard
description: Removed the Travel Mug Ember card from the mobile Lovelace dashboard and validated the dashboard JSON after the edit.
close_reason: Completed: removed the Travel Mug card from the mobile dashboard and verified the dashboard file still contains valid JSON.

value: Bathroom devices are Matter/Thread, NOT Zigbee. Bathroom Lamp is Nanoleaf Essentials A19 (Matter, fw 3.5.10 — behind others at 4.1.3, update via Nanoleaf app). Bathroom Presence Sensor is Aqara FP300 (Matter/Thread). Both on same Matter config entry.

Thread border routers are Apple devices (HomePods + Apple TV), NOT eero. The eero has its own separate Thread network (eero-thread-a2f5) but the HA Matter devices use the Apple Thread network (MyHome1620262151). 4 TBRs: Living Room, Kitchen, Bedroom (all Apple), and Shapes B178 (Nanoleaf). WiFi disruptions impact Thread because the Apple TBRs connect to HA over WiFi.
